attacking_style: Attacks from breakaways on final steep climbs, often coordinated with teammates. Prefers single decisive effort rather than sustained multi-climb battles, typically launching on the ultimate ascent.
team_tactics: Relies heavily on teammate setup — O'Connor and Dewulf placed him perfectly for stage wins. When isolated (Movistar days), struggled to sustain attacks against organized chase groups.
terrain_preference: Single steep summit finishes at altitude where pure climbing power dominates. Exposed badly on time trials and multi-climb stages requiring sustained efforts across varied terrain.
vulnerability: Time trials destroy his GC ambitions (lost 46sec in 8-minute gap at Tour de Suisse). Cannot follow elite climbers across multiple efforts or when pace is relentless rather than explosive.
key_patterns: Breakaway specialist who wins from reduced groups on final climb, not GC contention (3 observations)
Teammate coordination essential: DEWULF and O'CONNOR setups led to wins; isolation led to failures (4 observations)
Time trial liability severe enough to collapse GC prospects within single stage (2 observations)
Form peaks strategically: Tour de Suisse GC run directly preceded Tour stage win (2 observations)
Single-climb power elite, but multi-climb days expose inability to match top GC riders (3 observations)
form_trajectory: positive: Won Tour de Suisse stage, took yellow, then won Tour de France stage weeks later showing sustained peak timing
